Patch Set 12: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 12:

When retriggering a patch for testing, please put a comment into the bug that this is being done and what Jira ticket is tracking the original failure. Otherwise, nobody knows why a test failed, whether caused by some unrelated failure or by a problem in this patch.

In either case, tracking the test failures is important so that we can focus efforts in the right place. We don't want to land a patch that is itself passing only a fraction of the time and introduces an intermittent failure into master. Conversely, if some tests are failing for unrelated reasons we need to know the reason and track the number of times it is hit so that we can improve our test and code reliability.

Patch Set 12: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/871f46ea-f56c-11e2-a8f6-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  No failures.

commit.subject: LU-3342 mdt: HSM coordinator agent interface
commit.message:
LU-3342 mdt: HSM coordinator agent interface

To move data with external storage, HSM coordinator
uses a Copy Tool running on a client named agent.
This patch implements the interface for these agents.

Signed-off-by: JC Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I58773a7a975458de15d332f2d4b97988f92cd902
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6534
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Tested-by: Hudson
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
